_comment_purpose: Project-level Copilot hooks for tui-translator. These hooks supplement (not replace) the global ~/.copilot/hooks/hooks.json. They run on every preToolUse event and gate bash commands, file edits, and task completion.
_comment_execution: Both `bash` and `powershell` keys are provided so the hooks work on Linux/macOS (bash) and Windows (powershell). Python stdlib only — no third-party packages required.
_comment_maintenance: To disable a hook temporarily, add `"disabled": true` to its entry. To understand what each hook does, read the docstring at the top of the corresponding .py file.

preToolUse.comment: GUARD 1 — Dangerous shell commands: blocks sudo/runas, rm -rf on root, force-push (--force), download-pipe-execute, multi-commit hard reset, SQL DROP. Normal cargo/git operations are not affected. See dangerous-commands.py for full list.

GUARD 2 — Hardcoded secrets: blocks any edit or create that contains a real Google API key, Google service-account private key, AWS key, GitHub token, PEM private key block, or JWT token. Placeholder values (YOUR_KEY_HERE, example, dummy) are allowed. Protects against accidental credential leakage — a leaked Google key causes unexpected API billing.

GUARD 3 — Rust/Cargo/CI coding standards: (a) .rs files: unsafe{} must have // SAFETY: comment, dbg!() is banned, .unwrap() requires justification comment outside test code; (b) Cargo.toml: wildcard deps ("*") and deep path deps are banned; (c) .github/workflows/*.yml: continue-on-error: true needs a comment, echoing secrets to logs is banned.

GUARD 4 — Commit safety gate: runs only on `git commit`. Blocks commits that include: (a) dbg!() macros in staged .rs files; (b) credential files (.env, google-credentials*.json, *.pem, *.key, *.p12); (c) binary files over 1 MB (use Git LFS instead); (d) todo!() macros with no GitHub issue reference. Normal commits are not affected.

GUARD 5 — Verification evidence gate: runs only on task_complete. Requires a `.copilot-state/cargo-test-pass` evidence marker that is less than 2 hours old when Rust source files have been modified. This enforces the Layer-1 check from docs/04-verification-plan.md: build must succeed and all unit tests must pass before a task is declared done. Create the marker after `cargo test` passes: PowerShell: `[System.DateTimeOffset]::UtcNow.ToUnixTimeSeconds() | Set-Content .copilot-state\cargo-test-pass`
